description: Issue #187: Improve font metrics precision for near-threshold PDFs

userStories.title: Extract glyph widths from font descriptors and /Widths arrays
userStories.description: Many PDFs include per-font /Widths arrays and /FontDescriptor entries with precise glyph metrics. Currently these are not always utilized, causing cumulative x-drift on pages with proportional fonts. Parse /Widths, /FirstChar, /LastChar from font dictionaries and use them for char width calculation.
acceptanceCriteria: Font /Widths arrays are parsed and used when available
No regression on PDFs already at 100%
userStories.notes: Key file: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs (font setup). /Widths is an array of widths for char codes from /FirstChar to /LastChar. The width for code c is Widths[c - FirstChar]. This should override standard font fallback widths. Check interpreter's resolve_char_width() or equivalent function.

userStories.title: Improve font descriptor ascent/descent handling
userStories.description: Some PDFs have vertical coordinate offsets because ascent/descent values from /FontDescriptor are not being used. The parser falls back to default ascent/descent (-250/1000) instead of reading /Ascent and /Descent from the font descriptor. Fix by extracting these values when available.
acceptanceCriteria: Font descriptor /Ascent and /Descent values are used when available
Vertical coordinate accuracy improves for PDFs with font descriptors
userStories.notes: Key file: crates/pdfplumber-parse/src/interpreter.rs. FontDescriptor has /Ascent, /Descent, /CapHeight, /Flags, /FontBBox, /ItalicAngle, /StemV. The ascent/descent affect the top/bottom bbox calculation for each character. Check how char bbox vertical coordinates are computed and where font descriptor values should be injected.
